channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Political Narratives on Charter Change Amid Senate Instability
item.description: The recent narrative pushed by Senate President Alan Peter Cayetano and Senator Imee Marcos about charter change in the Philippines faces significant political challenges. Despite claims linking Senat...

item.title: Penticton invests in CCTV and policing hub to enhance waterfront safety
item.description: The City of Penticton is taking proactive steps to improve safety along Lakeshore Drive, one of the busiest waterfront areas on Okanagan Lake. City council approved nearly $130,000 in funding for enha...

item.title: U.S. Nonprofits Respond to Government Pressure and Threats
item.description: In recent years, social scientists have highlighted concerns about the rise of authoritarian strategies in democratic countries, often referred to as the “authoritarian playbook.” Although no official...

item.title: Orlando Pirates Aim for Premiership Glory in Season Finale Against Orbit College
item.description: Orlando Pirates are gearing up for a high-stakes clash against Orbit College at Mbombela Stadium in the final round of the Betway Premiership. Assistant coach Mandla Ncikazi has emphasised the importa...

item.title: Montreal Canadiens Show Remarkable Performance in Playoffs with Youthful Energy
item.description: The Montreal Canadiens are demonstrating a remarkable playoff performance, reminiscent of their 1971, 1986, and 1993 Stanley Cup-winning teams. Despite facing a dominant Carolina Hurricanes squad in t...

item.title: Street Life and Culture in 1970s New York City
item.description: The 1970s in New York City were a period marked by striking contrasts between urban decline and cultural innovation. Known as “Fear City,” the metropolis struggled with a severe fiscal crisis, which l...

item.title: Delhi Government to Focus on Treated Wastewater for Yamuna Instead of Ganga Diversion
item.description: The Delhi government’s proposal to divert 500 cusecs of water from the Ganga to the Yamuna, initiated last year under a central project to rejuvenate the Yamuna, is now facing major feasibility challe...

item.title: CMAT condemns fascism and calls on artists to take a stand at Ivor Novello Awards
item.description: Irish musician CMAT used her acceptance speech at the 2026 Ivor Novello Awards in London to address pressing political issues, urging fellow artists to take a stand rather than remain neutral. Winning...

item.title: Pendlebury Reaches 433 Games as North Melbourne Fights Gold Coast at Marvel Stadium
item.description: On Saturday, AFL fans were treated to an action-packed day of footy with Scott Pendlebury preparing to break the club games record at the MCG. The Collingwood veteran reached his milestone, marking a ...

item.title: Nigerian man sentenced in US for defrauding Alaska PFD programme
item.description: One Nigerian man, Adepoju Salako, wey dey live for Philadelphia, Pennsylvania, don land jail for one and half years for United States because of wire fraud wey e carry out for 2022 Permanent Fund Divi...

item.title: Behind the Scenes of Rocky's Iconic Final Fight
item.description: The 1976 film 'Rocky', directed by John G. Avildsen and written by Sylvester Stallone, became iconic not just for its story but for the ingenuity of its final fight scene between Rocky Balboa and Apol...

item.title: Kenya Remains Ebola-Free as Government Boosts Health Surveillance and Preparedness
item.description: Health CS Aden Duale has reassured Kenyans that there are no confirmed Ebola cases in the country after testing three suspected patients and four contacts, all of whom returned negative results. This ...

item.title: Quantum Foods anticipates tougher trading conditions in second half due to fuel costs and global supply chain pressures
item.description: Quantum Foods Holdings, a major South African poultry group, reported strong results for the six months ending March 31, 2026, but has warned that the second half of the year may present significant c...

item.title: Ebuka and Banky W discuss their past addictions and journey to recovery
item.description: For latest episode of Mentality with Ebuka, Ebuka Obi-Uchendu and Banky W open up about their struggles with addiction. Ebuka talk say he start to dey smoke when he dey JSS3 because of peer pressure, ...

item.title: Batangas Lawmaker Distributes Rice to Families Missing Government Aid
item.description: Batangas 1st District Representative Leandro Legarda Leviste announced on May 22 that he will personally distribute one million sacks of rice to families in his district who have not yet received gove...

item.title: UBTH Celebrates Prof. Omoti’s Election as NMA President with Dinner
item.description: The University of Benin Teaching Hospital (UBTH) don organise celebratory dinner to honour Prof. Afekhide Ernest Omoti, wey recently become the new president of the Nigerian Medical Association (NMA)....

item.title: U.S. temporarily restricts green-card holders from Ebola-affected regions
item.description: The U.S. government announced on Friday a temporary travel restriction that prevents green-card holders from re-entering the country if they have visited the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda, or S...

item.title: Government plans to redistribute idle MeTL tea estates to support smallholder farmers
item.description: The Tanzanian government don start talks to take over more than 2,000 hectares of idle tea estates wey belong to Mohamed Enterprises Limited (MeTL) for Mbeya Region. Deputy Minister for Agriculture, D...

item.title: Gas Explosion at Shanxi Coal Mine Claims Eight Lives and Traps 38 Miners
item.description: A gas explosion at the Liushenyu coal mine in Changzhi city, Shanxi province, northern China, has resulted in the deaths of at least eight miners and left 38 others trapped underground. The incident o...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa Faces ICC Warrant Amid Evade Attempts
item.description: Senator Bato dela Rosa is reportedly evading an International Criminal Court (ICC) warrant related to his alleged involvement in crimes against humanity. According to reports, within a single week, De...
